title: VimFu — :set wrap / nowrap
description: Toggle line wrapping with :set wrap and :set nowrap in Vim.

setup.content:
# Demo
def greet(name):
    return f"Hello {name}, welcome to the world of Vim editing and text processing!"

result = greet("World")
print(result)

steps.say: By default, Vim wraps long lines to fit the screen. You can toggle this with set wrap and set nowrap.
With nowrap, long lines extend beyond the screen edge. You have to scroll horizontally to see the rest.
Now let's turn wrapping back on.
The long line wraps to the next screen row. The text is still one line, it just displays across multiple rows.
